Response: Inria (French National Institute for Research in Digital Science and Technology), Paris; LAAS-CNRS (Laboratoire d'Analyse et d'Architecture des Systèmes, Toulouse); primary developer: Justin Carpentier (Inria Willow team); co-creator: Nicolas Mansard (LAAS-CNRS Gepetto team)

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Fast and flexible C++ library for rigid-body dynamics computations and their analytical derivatives; implements state-of-the-art algorithms (RNEA, CRBA, ABA, JSIM) for computing kinematics, dynamics, and Jacobians of articulated rigid-body models; includes Python bindings and supports robotics, animation, and optimal control applications

Response: yes (name evokes speed — 'pinocchio' as benchmark character; heavily templated for compile-time optimization; benchmarked as fastest open-source rigid-body dynamics library; supports ABA algorithm for O(n) forward dynamics)

column4: Pinocchio is among the fastest open-source rigid-body dynamics libraries available. Supports URDF and SRDF model loading. Python bindings (pip install pin) work only on Linux currently. Conda install (conda install pinocchio -c conda-forge) works on all platforms. Widely used in whole-body control, trajectory optimization, and learning-for-control research. Integral to many Inria/LAAS robotics frameworks (HPP, tsid, crocoddyl).
